description: Existing dominant capitation/PHO/contracting architecture with constrained upstream expansion.
Improve allocation of capitation without changing supply architecture.
Add top-level access target to reweighted capitation.
Defined contact-type benefits, demand-driven within rules, retaining capitation for continuity.
Allow eligible activity by GPs, NPs, pharmacists, allied health, paramedics and other accredited providers within scope.
Benefits schedule plus scope governance, equity protections, direct/optional claiming, ambulance alternatives, PHO-function reform, data, KPIs and audit.

option: Loose demand-driven benefits with weak controls
description: High activity funding with weak scope, audit, equity, data and co-payment controls.
Strengthen ambulance alternatives and ACC/prehospital funding, without primary care architecture reform.
Reduce PHO payment intermediation and allow direct rules-based claiming, without comprehensive benefits/scope reform.
Prioritise hospital capacity and acute rescue while leaving upstream access constrained.
